question_text: Spring tides occur when the Sun and Moon are aligned on the same side of Earth. What is the main reason these tides are higher than usual?

options.label: A
options.text: The Sun and Moon's gravitational forces combine to pull more strongly on Earth's oceans

options.label: B
options.text: The Earth moves closer to the Sun during spring

options.label: C
options.text: The Moon's orbit brings it very close to Earth

options.label: D
options.text: The temperature of the oceans increases in spring

explanation: Spring tides occur when the gravitational pulls of the Sun and Moon act in the same direction, creating a stronger combined pull on Earth's oceans. This happens twice per month during full and new moon phases.
